title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
desempenho_vs_baseline: HumanEval Python improves from GPT-4 80.1% pass@1 to 91.0% (+10.9pp); HumanEval Rust improves from GPT-4 60.0% to 68.0% (+8.0pp); LeetcodeHard Python improves from GPT-4 7.5% to 15.0% (+7.5pp).

title: Self-Taught Optimizer (STOP): Recursively Self-Improving Code Generation
desempenho_vs_baseline: Improved improvers outperform the seed improver on all transfer tasks reported in Table 1; on LPN, the paper reports consistent mean downstream performance improvement with GPT-4.

title: Empowering Agile-Based Generative Software Development through Human-AI Teamwork
desempenho_vs_baseline: AgileGen GPT3.5 outperforms ChatDev_24 GPT3.5 by +0.089 CodeBLEU and +16.4pp Pass@1; AgileGen GPT4 outperforms GPT4o+CoT by +0.105 CodeBLEU and +24.7pp Pass@1.

title: FALCON: Feedback-driven Adaptive Long/short-term memory reinforced Coding OptimizatioN
desempenho_vs_baseline: HumanEval improves from RLTF 76.8 to 82.9 (+6.1pp); MBPP improves from RLTF 75.9 to 80.7 (+4.8pp); APPS all pass@1 improves from RLTF 3.27 to 3.50 (+0.23pp).

title: LLMs as Continuous Learners: Improving the Reproduction of Defective Code in Software Issues
all_delta_candidates: signed_percentage_points: +20.00pp | from_to_percent: from 34.00% to 54.00%; from 33.33% to 53.33%; from 22.06% to 26.47%; from 23.53% to 26.47%
desempenho_vs_baseline: Compared with CodeR, reproduction accuracy improves from 34.00% to 54.00% LLM-judged (+20.00pp) and from 33.33% to 53.33% human-judged (+20.00pp). AutoCodeRover fix rate improves from 22.06% to 26.47%; Agentless from 23.53% to 26.47%.

title: Skill-Adpative Imitation Learning for UI Test Reuse
desempenho_vs_baseline: SAIL-DeepSeek-V2 improves over CraftDroid 25.5% to 63.6% overall (+38.1pp; reported as 149% higher SR). In non-1-to-1 mappings, SAIL-GPT-4o reaches 55.6% versus CraftDroid 14.1% (reported as 294% improvement).

title: A Self-Improving Coding Agent
all_delta_candidates: signed_percentage_points: +36pp | from_to_percent: from 17% to 53%; from 0.82 to 0.96; from 0.35 to 0.43; from 0.65 to 0.71
desempenho_vs_baseline: SWE-Bench Verified improves from 17% to 53% at the best reported iteration (+36pp). File editing improves from 0.82 to 0.96 at peak, symbol location from 0.35 to 0.43 at peak, and LiveCodeBench from 0.65 to 0.71 by iteration 15.

title: AccelOpt: A Self-Improving LLM Agentic System for AI Accelerator Kernel Optimization
desempenho_vs_baseline: +12pp throughput médio no Trainium 1 (49%→61%); +14pp no Trainium 2 (45%→59%) vs kernels iniciais; equivalente a Claude Sonnet 4

title: Agent KB: Leveraging Cross-Domain Experience for Agentic Problem Solving
benchmark_markers: SWE-bench Lite; GAIA; Humanity's Last Exam; GPQA
desempenho_vs_baseline: SWE-Agent GPT-4.1 improves 24.3% to 38.0% at 50 iterations (+13.7pp) and 27.0% to 42.3% at 100 iterations (+15.3pp). OpenHands Claude-3.7 improves 30.0% to 51.0% at 50 iterations (+21.0pp) and 41.3% to 53.3% at 100 iterations (+12.0pp).

title: AlphaOPT: Formulating Optimization Programs with Self-Improving LLM Experience Library
benchmark_markers: NLP4LP; NL4OPT; IndustryOR; MAMO/ComplexLP; LogiOR; OptiBench
desempenho_vs_baseline: AlphaOPT exceeds ExpeL on test micro/macro averages (80.1/76.4 vs 69.9/66.3) and out-of-distribution micro/macro averages (86.5/74.8 vs 74.3/64.1). Removing refinement reduces out-of-distribution macro from 74.8 to 70.5.

title: CodeDistiller: Automatically Generating Code Libraries for Scientific Coding Agents
desempenho_vs_baseline: Downstream A/B preference proportions favor CodeDistiller over the generic-example baseline for accuracy (0.50 vs 0.26), completeness (0.46 vs 0.16), and soundness (0.50 vs 0.30), with ties making up the remainder.

title: Confucius Code Agent: Scalable Agent Scaffolding for Real-World Codebases
desempenho_vs_baseline: +3.0pp vs OpenAI (59.0% vs 56.0%) com GPT-5.2; +6.9pp vs Live-SWE-Agent (52.7% vs 45.8%) com Claude 4.5 Sonnet; +1.8pp vs OpenHands (74.6% vs 72.8%) no SWE-Bench-Verified

title: Darwin Godel Machine: Open-Ended Evolution of Self-Improving Agents
desempenho_vs_baseline: DGM outperforms variants without self-improvement and without open-ended exploration; best discovered SWE-bench agent is comparable to checked open-source human-designed SoTA, while Polyglot improves 14.2% to 30.7% on the full benchmark.

title: EvoLattice: Persistent Internal-Population Evolution through Multi-Alternative Quality-Diversity Graph Representations for LLM-Guided Program Discovery
desempenho_vs_baseline: EvoLattice exceeds AlphaEvolve-style diff evolution (0.11 ±0.06), ShinkaEvolve-like parent sampling (0.12 ±0.05), FunSearch-style proxy generation (0.09 ±0.07), and LPZero (0.10 ±0.05).

title: Evolving Triple Knowledge-Augmented LLMs for Code Translation in Repository Context
desempenho_vs_baseline: For Claude-3-5-Sonnet, K3 Trans improves over prompt engineering from 57.9% to 75.7% Compilation@1 and from 49.1% to 67.7% Pass@1; the paper reports relative improvements up to 135.9% on Pass@1 and 32.8% on CodeBLEU among studied LLMs.

title: EXPEREPAIR: Dual-Memory Enhanced LLM-based Repository-Level Program Repair
desempenho_vs_baseline: ExpeRepair surpasses PatchPilot 45.3% and DARS 47.0% on SWE-bench Lite while costing far less than DARS ($2.07 vs $12.24). Removing the experience module drops resolved rate from 47.7% to 41.3%.

title: From Knowledge to Noise: CTIM-Rover and the Pitfalls of Episodic Memory in Software Engineering Agents
desempenho_vs_baseline: -2pp vs baseline (40% vs 42%); CTIM only -11pp (31% vs 42%)

title: Git Context Controller: Manage the Context of LLM-based Agents like Git
desempenho_vs_baseline: +6.2pp Pass@1 vs Folding Agent (melhor baseline) com Claude 4 Sonnet em SWE-Bench Verified (0.802 vs 0.740); +1.9pp BrowseComp-Plus com GPT-5 (0.834 vs 0.815)

title: Huxley-Gödel Machine: Human-Level Coding Agent Development by an Approximation of the Optimal Self-Improving Machine
desempenho_vs_baseline: HGM requires 517 CPU-hours versus DGM 1231 on SWE-Verified-60, and 347 versus DGM 2385 on Polyglot; on SWE-Lite standard, the HGM best-belief agent reaches 49.0% with GPT-5-mini and 57.0% with GPT-5 transfer.

title: Improving Code Localization with Repository Memory
desempenho_vs_baseline: +4.9% Acc@5 absoluto vs LocAgent em SWE-bench-verified; +3.1% em SWE-bench-live; resolve rate +3.4% em end-to-end (40.4% vs 37.0%)

title: Lingxi: Repository-Level Issue Resolution Framework Enhanced by Procedural Knowledge Guided Scaling
desempenho_vs_baseline: With Claude 4 Sonnet, Lingxi reports 74.60% versus OpenHands and Augment Agent prior at 70.40%, SWE-search at 70.08%, SWE-agent at 66.60%, and mini-SWE-agent at 64.93%. With Kimi-K2, Lingxi reports 70.33% versus OpenHands 65.40% and mini-SWE-agent 43.80%.

title: Multi-Agent Code-Orchestrated Generation for Reliable Infrastructure-as-Code
desempenho_vs_baseline: GPT-5 MACOG reaches 74.02 IaC-Eval, BLEU 11.86, CodeBERTScore 80.54, and LLM-judge 94.10. Ablations reduce IaC-Eval, including -Engineer at 64.89, -Security Prover at 61.45, -DevOps at 56.93, and -Memory Curator at 72.17.

title: ReasoningBank: Scaling Agent Self-Evolving with Reasoning Memory
desempenho_vs_baseline: On SWE-bench Verified, Gemini-2.5-flash ReasoningBank reports 38.8% resolve versus 34.2% No Memory and 35.4% Synapse; Gemini-2.5-pro reports 57.4% versus 54.0% No Memory and 53.4% Synapse. Failure-trajectory ablations report larger gains than success-only memory variants.

title: Reflection-Driven Control for Trustworthy Code Agents
desempenho_vs_baseline: Base+Reflex improves security rate by +2.9 for gpt-3.5-turbo, +9.3 for gpt-4o, +11.2 for qwen3-coder-plus, and +9.1 for gemini-2.5-pro; pass rate is mostly preserved but decreases for qwen3-coder-plus (86.7 to 80.1).

title: Smarter Together: Creating Agentic Communities of Practice through Shared Experiential Learning
desempenho_vs_baseline: +0.66 code quality Qwen3-Coder (4.23→4.89); +0.41 Haiku 4.5 (4.50→4.91); +0.05 GPT-5-Codex (4.78→4.83); todos WITH-SPARK excedem qualidade humana (4.28)

title: SWE-Exp: Experience-Driven Software Issue Resolution
desempenho_vs_baseline: +2.2pp vs SWE-Search com Claude 4 Sonnet (73.0% vs 70.8%); +6.6pp vs SWE-Search com DeepSeek (42.0% vs 35.4%); +3.2pp vs SWE-Agent com DeepSeek (42.0% vs 38.8%); +4.2pp vs EvoCoder com DeepSeek (42.0% vs 38.0%)

title: TAI3: Testing Agent Integrity in Interpreting User Intent
desempenho_vs_baseline: Table 1 reports broad EESR gains over SelfRef, including Finance GPT-4o-mini VALID 41.5 to 61.0 (+19.5), Healthcare GPT-4o-mini INVALID 44.7 to 57.4 (+12.7), and Office Llama-3.1-8B UNDERSPEC 65.7 to 82.0 (+16.3).

title: TALM: Dynamic Tree-Structured Multi-Agent Framework with Long-Term Memory for Scalable Code Generation
desempenho_vs_baseline: +3.17pp BigCodeBench vs Direct (53.27% vs 50.10% GPT-4o); +5.00pp ClassEval class vs Direct (38.00% vs 33.00%); +2.00pp ClassEval class vs MapCoder (38.00% vs 36.00%); ~50% menos tokens que MapCoder

title: TOM-SWE: User Mental Modeling For Software Engineering Agents
desempenho_vs_baseline: +11.5pp Ambiguous SWE-bench (63.4% vs 51.9%) com Claude Sonnet 4; +41.6pp Stateful SWE (59.7% vs 18.1%); satisfaction 3.62 vs 2.57 (+41%); RAGCodeAct inferior ao ToMCodeAct em todos modelos

title: Toward Generation of Test Cases from Task Descriptions via History-aware Planning
desempenho_vs_baseline: On challenging MiniWoB++ tasks, HxAgent averages 82% Exact-Match versus 10% for Li et al. (GPT-4o). On real-world applications it averages 87% Exact-Match versus 28% and 93% Prefix-Match versus 34%. Removing experience drops Exact-Match from 97% to 88%; removing both SAM and experience drops it to 47%.

title: Xolver: Multi-Agent Reasoning with Holistic Experience Learning Just Like an Olympiad Team
desempenho_vs_baseline: +18.6pp AIME'24 avg vs o3-mini-medium LongCoT (93.8% vs 75.8%); +13.6pp AIME'25 (89.4% vs 75.8%); +21.0pp LiveCodeBench (87.3% vs 66.3%); supera o4-mini-high em AIME'24 (93.8% vs 93.4%) e LiveCodeBench (87.3% vs 69.5%)

title: AdaExplore: Failure-Driven Adaptation and Diversity-Preserving Search for Efficient Kernel Generation
desempenho_vs_baseline: At 50 steps on Level-2, AdaExplore reaches 2.65x speedup versus IR with skill memory at 2.59x and PS with skill memory at 2.12x. Removing cross-task skill memory drops speedup from 2.65x to 2.32x and Fast@1.2 from 71% to 56%.

title: AgentDevel: Reframing Self-Evolving LLM Agents as Release Engineering
benchmark_markers: SWE-bench Verified; SWE-bench Lite; StableToolBench; WebArena
desempenho_vs_baseline: On SWE-bench Lite, AgentDevel reaches 22.0% versus base 11.0% and reported SWE-agent 18.0%. On WebArena ablation, the full pipeline keeps P2F regression rate at 3.1% with zero bad releases; removing the flip gate raises P2F to 14.8% and creates four bad releases.

title: AgentGA: Evolving Code Solutions in Agent-Seed Space
desempenho_vs_baseline: Parent-conditioned operators win 48.7% of parent-child tournaments overall, while Initial proposals win only 12.2%. Continue wins 148/286 tournaments, Merge 68/104, EDA 267/593, Ablation 34/78, and Initial 9/74.

title: APEX-EM: Non-Parametric Online Learning for Autonomous Agents via Structured Procedural-Episodic Experience Replay
benchmark_markers: BigCodeBench; ALFWorld; Humanity's Last Exam; KGQAGen-10k; Lifelong Agent Bench
desempenho_vs_baseline: BigCodeBench improvement is +29.4pp over no memory and exceeds MemRL's reported +11.0pp gain. KGQA full-memory plus Opus judge reaches 89.6% versus 41.3% no memory. HLE entity graph retrieval improves 25.2% to 48.0%.

title: BEAM: Bi-level Memory-adaptive Algorithmic Evolution for LLM-Powered Heuristic Design
desempenho_vs_baseline: In CVRP, BEAM with Split and local search reports gaps of 0.09%, 0.38%, and 0.86% for CVRP-100/200/500 versus EoH gaps of 0.22%, 0.57%, and 1.09%. In BBOB, BEAM average gap is 0.007 versus ReEvo 0.957, EoH 6.032, and LLaMEA-HPO 0.386.

title: Beyond Crash-to-Patch: Patch Evolution for Linux Kernel Repair
desempenho_vs_baseline: Baseline repair succeeds on 1/6 bugs with CodeBERTScore 0.42 and 83.3% compile success. Memory-guided repair succeeds on 4/6 with CodeBERTScore 0.74. Fine-tuned Gemma-3-12B PatchAdvisor succeeds on 5/6 with average rank 2.7.

title: Co-RedTeam: Orchestrated Security Discovery and Exploitation with LLM Agents
all_delta_candidates: from_to_percent: from 59.1% to 50.0%; from 60.0% to 40.0%; from 31.5% to 22.6%
desempenho_vs_baseline: For Gemini-3-pro, OpenHands reaches 45.2%/45.0%/5.0%/20.2% across CyBench, BountyBench exploit/detect, and CyberGym; Co-RedTeam reaches 63.7%/65.0%/20.0%/37.3%. Removing memory drops Gemini-2.5-pro CyBench from 59.1% to 50.0%, BountyBench exploit from 60.0% to 40.0%, and CyberGym from 31.5% to 22.6%.

title: Codebase-Memory: Tree-Sitter-Based Knowledge Graphs for LLM Code Exploration via MCP
desempenho_vs_baseline: MCP Agent reaches 90% of Explorer quality while using 10 times fewer tokens and 2.1 times fewer tool calls; it matches or exceeds explorer behavior on graph-native hub/caller queries for 19 of 31 languages.

title: CoEvoSkills: Self-Evolving Agent Skills via Co-Evolutionary Verification
desempenho_vs_baseline: CoEvoSkills improves +40.5pp over no-skill and +17.6pp over human-curated skills. Skill-Creator reaches 34.1%, single-session generation 32.4%, SkillsBench self-generated 32.0%, and CoT-guided self-generation 30.7%. Removing the surrogate verifier drops pass rate from 71.1% to 41.1%; removing evolution drops to 48.6%.

title: Complementary Reinforcement Learning
desempenho_vs_baseline: Multi-task table reports baseline average 0.75, Complementary RL evaluated with experience 0.82, and Complementary RL without experience 0.78. Static Online Experience with experience averages 0.59 and without experience 0.54, showing that static experience alone is insufficient.

title: Controlled Self-Evolution for Algorithmic Code Optimization
desempenho_vs_baseline: With GPT-5, CSE improves average MI to 67.47% versus Direct 59.13%, Self-Reflection 60.24%, SE-Agent 64.41%, and AlphaEvolve 63.04%. Ablation on GPT-5 shows full CSE MI 68.10% versus 63.08% without memory, 63.82% without planning, and 64.72% without evolution.

title: CORAL: Towards Autonomous Multi-Agent Evolution for Open-Ended Discovery
all_delta_candidates: from_to_percent: from 1350 to 1601; from 80.2 to 77.3; from 4566 to 4444
desempenho_vs_baseline: CORAL improvement rates are reported as 3-10 times higher than fixed evolutionary-search baselines and typically converge within 5-20 evaluations versus 60-100 for baselines. Knowledge ablation worsens Kernel Engineering from 1350 to 1601 cycles, Polyominoes from 80.2 to 77.3, and Txn Scheduling from 4566 to 4444.

title: DARWIN: Dynamic Agentically Rewriting Self-Improving Network
desempenho_vs_baseline: The paper reports a 2.07 percent reduction in perplexity and a 1.26 percent reduction in MFU; removing persistent memory produced about 3 percent worse performance, but the authors caution that the results are not statistically meaningful.

title: Decocted Experience Improves Test-Time Inference in LLM Agents
benchmark_markers: SWE-bench Verified; SWE-bench full training set; WebShop; MATH/MATH500
desempenho_vs_baseline: On WebShop and SWE, lesson-based context consistently outperforms raw trajectories and vanilla context-free agents. Concept-tree retrieval improves over top-k lesson retrieval on WebShop and shows a noticeable improvement on SWE, while excessive K/raw context can degrade performance.

title: DomAgent: Leveraging Knowledge Graphs and Case-Based Reasoning for Domain-Specific Code Generation
all_delta_candidates: from_to_percent: from 39.62% to 96.64%; from 71.22% to 98.04%; from 29.3% to 39.2%; from 51.0% to 58.6%
desempenho_vs_baseline: On Truck CAN Signal, Qwen2.5-7B improves from 39.62% to 96.64% total pass@1, and GPT-4o with DomRetriever improves from 71.22% to 98.04%. On DS-1000, Qwen2.5-7B improves from 29.3% to 39.2% total pass@1; external GPT-4o improves from 51.0% to 58.6% with LLaMA3.1-8B DomRetriever.

title: EET: Experience-Driven Early Termination for Cost-Efficient Software Engineering Agents
desempenho_vs_baseline: +2.7% resolve rate médio vs agentes sem EET; Agentless beneficia mais (+7.5% abs com GPT-5-mini)

title: EffiSkill: Agent Skill Based Automated Code Efficiency Optimization
desempenho_vs_baseline: With GPT-5-mini, EffiSkill reaches Python OPT@1/OPT@8 of 26.48/37.40 and C++ OPT@1/OPT@8 of 44.62/66.77. With Qwen3-Coder, it reaches Python 21.35/36.60 and C++ 34.19/56.50, outperforming Instruction, RAG, CoT, SBLLM, FasterPy, and EffiCoder where applicable.

title: FailureMem: A Failure-Aware Multimodal Framework for Autonomous Software Repair
desempenho_vs_baseline: FailureMem reaches 31.1%, 33.1%, 33.3%, 32.5%, and 33.8% resolved rates with GPT-4.1, GPT-5.1, GPT-5.2, Claude 4, and Claude 4.5 respectively, compared with GUIRepair at 28.8%, 29.4%, 29.8%, 28.6%, and 31.5%.

title: KAPSO: A Knowledge-grounded framework for Autonomous Program Synthesis and Optimization
desempenho_vs_baseline: +15.56pp medal rate vs R&D-Agent em MLE-Bench; +30.1 ELO vs ALE-Agent em ALE-Bench; vantagem concentrada em tarefas Hard (+17.78pp)

title: KernelBlaster: Continual Cross-Task CUDA Optimization via Memory-Augmented In-Context Reinforcement Learning
desempenho_vs_baseline: GeoMean 1.43× (L1), 2.50× (L2), 1.50× (L3) vs PyTorch; 1.67× vs no_mem_agent; supera AI CUDA Engineer em fast_p para speedups moderados a altos

title: KernelSkill: A Multi-Agent Framework for GPU Kernel Optimization
desempenho_vs_baseline: Compared with STARK, KernelSkill improves average speedup by 79.5% on Level 1, 4.8% on Level 2, and 21.5% on Level 3. It reaches these gains with 15 refinement rounds, while STARK reports 30 rounds.

title: LatentMem: Customizing Latent Memory for Multi-Agent Systems
benchmark_markers: BigCodeBench; TriviaQA; StrategyQA; PopQA; PDDL; KodCode
all_delta_candidates: from_to_percent: from 52.86 to 61.82; from 53.38 to 61.00; from 53.37 to 60.04; from 52.80 to 60.06 | score_points: +18.45 points
desempenho_vs_baseline: In Table 4 with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, LatentMem improves AutoGen average from 52.86 to 61.82, MacNet from 53.38 to 61.00, CAMEL from 53.37 to 60.04, and DyLAN from 52.80 to 60.06; KodCode gains range from +14.90 to +18.45 points.

title: Learning to Commit: Generating Organic Pull Requests via Online Repository Memory
desempenho_vs_baseline: Table 2 reports File IoU improvements in three of four settings, including 80% versus 61% in seq-all and 80% versus 68% in par-bycat. Table 3 reports skill-agent win rates of 54%/57% in par-bycat and 55%/58% in seq-all under Claude/Gemini judges.

title: MEMCoder: Multi-dimensional Evolving Memory for Private-Library-Oriented Code Generation
desempenho_vs_baseline: With Qwen2.5-Coder, Naive RAG plus MEMCoder reaches 52.54 Pass@1 on NdonnxEval and 33.74 on NumbaEval versus Naive RAG at 27.22 and 23.16. In continual-learning comparison, MEMCoder reaches 33.74 Pass@1 on NumbaEval versus ReMem 23.16 and DC-RS 17.33.

title: MemCollab: Cross-Agent Memory Collaboration via Contrastive Trajectory Distillation
all_delta_candidates: from_to_percent: from 57.1 to 71.6; from 47.9 to 57.6; from 42.7 to 74.4; from 70.8 to 79.6
desempenho_vs_baseline: For Qwen2.5-7B, MemCollab improves average accuracy from 57.1 to 71.6; MBPP improves from 47.9 to 57.6 and HumanEval from 42.7 to 74.4. For Qwen2.5-32B, average accuracy improves from 70.8 to 79.6.

title: MemGovern: Enhancing Code Agents through Learning from Governed Human Experiences
desempenho_vs_baseline: +3.2pp com Claude 4 Sonnet (69.8% vs 66.6%); +8.2pp com Qwen3-235B (55.4% vs 47.2%); +9.4pp com GPT-4o (32.6% vs 23.2%); média +4.65pp em 7 LLMs

title: Memory Transfer Learning: How Memories are Transferred Across Domains in Coding Agents
benchmark_markers: SWE-bench Verified; LiveCodeBench; Polyglot/Aider-Polyglot; Terminal-Bench; ReplicationBench; MLGym-Bench
desempenho_vs_baseline: With GPT-5-mini, zero-shot average Pass@3 is 0.523 and MTL with Insight reaches 0.560. MTL outperforms ReasoningBank and AgentKB on the three-benchmark comparison with average 0.630 versus 0.601 and 0.613.

title: ODEL: An Experience-Augmented Self-Evolving Framework for Efficient Python-to-C++ Code Translation
desempenho_vs_baseline: ODEL External Experience outperforms UniTrans at 72.16% Pass@1 and 76.64% Pass@10. It also improves over ODEL Internal Experience by 6.35 Pass@1 points and 4.32 Pass@10 points.

title: OpenGame: Open Agentic Coding for Games
desempenho_vs_baseline: OpenGame with Claude Sonnet 4.6 outperforms Cursor with Claude Sonnet 4.6 by 5.6 Build Health, 5.8 Visual Usability, and 6.2 Intent Alignment points. GameCoder-27B reaches 63.9/57.0/54.1 and outperforms every direct LLM baseline on Build Health and Intent Alignment.

title: OpenSage: Self-programming Agent Generation Engine
benchmark_markers: SWE-Bench Pro; Terminal-Bench; CyberGym; DevOps-Gym; LoCoMo
desempenho_vs_baseline: On SWE-Bench Pro, SageAgent with Gemini 3 Flash reaches 59.0% versus SWE-agent 40.2% and Agentless 9.4%. In the memory ablation, OpenSage reaches 59.0 versus Mem0g 56.4 and NoMem 56.2.

title: Outcome-Conditioned Reasoning Distillation for Resolving Software Issues
all_delta_candidates: from_to_percent: from 27.3% to 37.7%; from 29.7% to 40.0%; from 27.0% to 35.6%
desempenho_vs_baseline: With GPT-4o, O-CRD improves over Agentless from 27.3% to 37.7% Pass@1 and exceeds SWE-Search by 6.7 percentage points. With GPT-5, it improves Agentless from 29.7% to 40.0%; with DeepSeek-V3, from 27.0% to 35.6%.

title: Procedural Knowledge at Scale Improves Reasoning
desempenho_vs_baseline: On LiveCodeBench V1-4 with Qwen3-32B and m=30, Reasoning Memory reaches 0.471 versus Length Scaling 0.405 and No RAG 0.387 at m=8; on LiveCodeBench V5-6 it reaches 0.508 versus Length Scaling 0.476 and No RAG 0.465.

title: ReCreate: Reasoning and Creating Domain Agents Driven by Experience
benchmark_markers: SWE-bench Verified; DS-1000; DA-Code; AppWorld; MATH/MATH500
desempenho_vs_baseline: On SWE subsets, ReCreate reaches 60.19 on Django and 63.64 on SymPy, exceeding the best baseline in each row. It also improves over ADAS and AgentSquare averages by more than seven points.

title: REVERE: Reflective Evolving Research Engineer for Scientific Workflows
desempenho_vs_baseline: REVERE improves SUPER Overall by +4.50 over Static SOTA, ResearchCodeBench Accuracy by +1.3, and ScienceAgentBench SuccessRate by +4.89; removing the full Global Training Context drops the representative SUPER Overall from 34.7 to 16.0.

title: RuleFlow: Generating Reusable Program Optimizations with LLMs
desempenho_vs_baseline: RuleFlow reaches a maximum speedup of 4.3x over DIAS and 1914.89x over MODIN; compared with a single-agent RuleGen variant, the multi-agent RuleGen obtains 68% valid rules versus 18%.

title: Scaling Teams or Scaling Time? Memory Enabled Lifelong Learning in LLM Multi-Agent Systems
desempenho_vs_baseline: In the coding topology ablation with Qwen3-32B-Instruct, local memory obtains TS 49.68, CS 46.69, and AAS 49.43 versus shared 46.79/45.96/48.62 and hybrid 46.52/40.32/46.92.

title: SEVerA: Verified Self-Evolving Agents
desempenho_vs_baseline: SEVerA exceeds the best constrained-decoding method on GSM-Symbolic by 21.3 percentage points and reports 52.6% on tau2-bench airline versus 47.3% for Agent-C with Claude Sonnet 4.5.

title: Software Self-Extension with SelfEvolve: an Agentic Architecture for Runtime Code Generation
desempenho_vs_baseline: SelfEvolve reaches 92.7% Pass@1 versus AutoGen at 30.9%, MetaGPT at 27.3%, and AgentCoder at 0.0%. Removing TDD reduces performance to 72.7% and raises average iterations from 2.2 to 4.7.

title: Structurally Aligned Subtask-Level Memory for Software Engineering Agents
desempenho_vs_baseline: +6.8pp Gemini 2.5 Pro (60.3% vs 53.5%); +5.6pp Gemini 2.5 Flash (41.9% vs 36.3%); +3.9pp Claude 3.7 Sonnet (56.1% vs 52.2%); +2.3pp Claude 4.0 Sonnet (65.8% vs 63.5%); média +4.7pp

title: TextBFGS: A Case-Based Reasoning Approach to Code Optimization via Error-Operator Retrieval
desempenho_vs_baseline: On HumanEval-Hard, TextBFGS reaches 97.78/93.33 versus 91.11/82.22 for TextBFGS without case base and 95.56/91.11 for REMO. Cross-domain TextBFGS maintains 74.36 MBPP Plus Pass using a HumanEval case base versus 58.12 for REMO.

title: Toward Autonomous Long-Horizon Engineering for ML Research
desempenho_vs_baseline: On PaperBench, AiScientist improves over IterAgent by 9.92 points with Gemini and 11.36 points with GLM-5. Removing File-as-Bus drops PaperBench by 6.41 points and MLE-Bench Lite any-medal rate by 31.82 points.

title: Traversal-as-Policy: Log-Distilled Gated Behavior Trees as Externalized, Verifiable Policies for Safe, Robust, and Efficient Agents
benchmark_markers: SWE-bench Verified; WebArena; GPQA; AgentSafetyBench; AgentHarm; Agent Security Bench
desempenho_vs_baseline: +39.0pp SWE-bench (73.6% vs 34.6%); +47.2pp WebArena (66.9% vs 19.7%); +28.5pp GPQA (87.3% vs 58.7%); violations 2.8%→0.2% SWE-bench; unsafe success 1.2%→0.0%

title: When the Specification Emerges: Benchmarking Faithfulness Loss in Long-Horizon Coding Agents
desempenho_vs_baseline: Single-shot outperforms emergent specification on 16 of 20 papers for Claude Code and 14 of 20 for Codex. ProjectGuard improves Claude IF50 on 15 of 20 papers; Codex semantic gains are small but DIR improves on 15 of 19 analyzed cases.

title: Your Code Agent Can Grow Alongside You with Structured Memory

title: Self-Adapting Agents for Automating Research Coding Workflows
desempenho_vs_baseline: SARE without ground truth improves the SUPER overall score to 38.8 versus 29.9 for the baseline, while SARE with ground truth reaches 52.8. Ablating the whole Global Training Context drops SUPER overall from 34.7 to 16.0 in the reported ablation table.
